reasoning: While the LLM fidelity signals indicate perfect visual and structural reproduction, programmatic accessibility checks identified a major table markup issue. Specifically, data values are incorrectly placed in `<th>` header cells, which misrepresents the data and disrupts screen reader navigation. This single major programmatic defect requires human review to correct the accessibility markup.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 3 table(s) have data values in <th> cells: "$ 47,756.04", "$ 12,400,720.08", "$ 57,272.02"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=3, datarow_as_header=0. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=1. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Fix table markup by changing <th> tags to <td> for cells containing data values like '$ 47,756.04'.
Ensure all tables have appropriate header rows using <th> tags.
